title: Parallelize similar.py index build (_index_track)
description: The similarity index pre-warm thread iterates every track and computes a 6-dim feature vector serially. Parallelize the per-track feature extraction; keep the index-dict assembly single-threaded behind _INDEX_LOCK.
acceptanceCriteria: On a 10k-track library, cold-start index build completes in <= 5 seconds (currently ~30s).
After build, similar._INDEX_BUILT is True and find_similar returns identical results to the v0 serial build (deterministic).
Concurrent calls to clear_index() and find_similar() are safe (lock semantics preserved).
Existing tests/test_similar.py cases stay green; the conftest similar.clear_index() fixture continues to reset state.

steps.details: In analysis/similar.py find _index_track(content, db) and the loop that calls it. Confirm _index_track is read-only (no writes to module state). If not, factor the read part into _extract_features(content, db) returning the 6-dim vector plus classification cache priming data.
In the index-build function (currently the loop), submit pool.futures for _extract_features per content. Collect into a results list as_completed.
Acquire _INDEX_LOCK once; populate _INDEX dict from collected results; set _INDEX_BUILT=True; release lock. This serializes all writes to module-global state.

Per CLAUDE.md, get_classification() is pre-warmed during _index_track so beam search lookup is O(1). If extraction in step 1 separated this priming out, call it inside the writer (after lock acquisition) so cache writes are also single-threaded. Or use per-thread cache writes if classify._class_cache writes are documented as thread-safe; default to single-threaded to be safe.
tests/test_similar.py: add a test that builds the index twice (clear in between) and asserts result equality for find_similar(track_id, n=10). Verify no flaky ordering.
RUN_PERF=1 pytest tests/perf/test_similar_index.py. Should reach <=5s on 10k.

technicalNotes: _INDEX, _INDEX_BUILT, _INDEX_LOCK are module-level; per CLAUDE.md, other modules must check _INDEX_BUILT via 'from . import similar as _similar_mod' to avoid stale copies.
Once TASK-015 lands, the per-track feature vector comes from the sidecar cache (microseconds) - this task's parallelism is mostly about cold-cache builds.
